evidence_sentence: Loss-of-function mutant of MOC1 exhibits a monoculm phenotype without tillers.
Overexpression of MOC1 leads to the formation of higher-order tillers such as tertiary, quaternary, and quinary.
moc3 mutant cannot form normal axillary meristems; severely reduced tillers.
Loss of MOC2 causes a significant reduction in tiller number.
lax1 single mutant shows reduced axillary bud formation.
lax2 single mutant: sparse panicle; reduced secondary branches and spikelets; fewer tillers.
lax1 lax2 double mutant: severe reduction in secondary branches and spikelets; dramatic tiller loss; 'All SBs and spikelets except terminal spikelets were absent'.
lax2 moc1 double mutant: complete loss of all branching except primary branches; monoculm.
tab1-1 produces no tillers even in 2-month-old shoots.
In fon2 mutant sizes of the SAM and inflorescence meristem were increased; axillary meristems increased.
In FON2 overexpressor, axillary meristem formation is inhibited.
tab1 fon2 double mutant produces a few tillers (partial rescue of tab1 tillerless phenotype).
d3 mutant is tillering dwarf with excessive tillers.
d14 mutant shows accelerated outgrowth of tillers; SL-insensitive.
d53 dominant mutant has total tiller number about three times that of wild type.
pActin::D53-GFP transgenic plants showed moderate increase in tillering.
d10 SL-biosynthesis mutant shows high-tillering dwarf phenotype.
d17/htd1 SL-biosynthesis mutant shows high-tillering dwarf phenotype.
d27 final tiller number is three times that of the wild type.
d88/d14L weak SL-insensitive phenotype with moderately increased tillers.
Os900 max1 mutant shows increased tillers.
fc1 mutant (OsTB1 loss-of-function) causes increase in the number of tillers; NIL-fc1 produces 143% more panicles.
Transgenic rice plants overexpressing OsTB1 exhibited markedly reduced lateral branching.
ipa1-1D: miR156 binding site mutation, IPA1 accumulation, reduced tiller number.
ipa1-2D novel allele generates large panicles and moderate panicle number, with reduced tillers.
ipa1-10 loss-of-function: more tillers, smaller panicles.
miR156 OE degrades OsSPL14 mRNA, resulting in increased tiller number.
miR529a OE increases tiller number by targeting OsSPL14.
Gn1a reduced-function allele: more tillers and more grains per panicle due to CK accumulation.
OsCKX2-RNAi: 27-81% increased panicle/tiller number vs wild-type.
OsCKX2-OE: reduced tillers (gene-dosage dependent).
osckx4 increases tillers via CK accumulation.
osckx9 mutant: increased tillers, decreased plant height and panicle size.
OsCKX9-OX: increased tillers, decreased height and panicle branches (paradox — CK depletion in node but altered SL response).
osckx11 increases tiller number and alters source-sink relationship.
OsIPT OE increases cytokinin levels; expected to promote tillering.
osipt4/7 loss reduces CK biosynthesis and expected reduced tiller outgrowth.
OsAHP-RNAi plants exhibited phenotypes expected for a deficiency in cytokinin signaling, including dwarfism and reduced tiller numbers.
ohk4 loss-of-function: reduced panicle, decreased higher-order tillers.
ngr5 loss-of-function mutant failed to induce tiller outgrowth in response to nitrogen.
NGR5-OE/high-expression allele: increased tiller number and yield.
Accessions with 29-bp insertion in OsTCP19 promoter (loss of expression) show high tillering response to nitrogen.
OsTCP19-OE: reduced tillers via DLT repression.
dlt mutant: dwarf and low tillering phenotype.
OsLBD37-OE: represses OsTCP19 and increases tillers.
Indica NRT1.1B allele in japonica background increases tiller number and NUE.
osnlp4 mutant: significantly decreased tiller number and plant height.
tad1 mutant showed an increased tiller number.
TAD1-overexpressing transgenic plants showed a reduced tiller number.
te mutant displays drastically increased tiller number (~3-fold).
TE-OE reduced tiller number.
te moc1-5 double: reduced tillers like moc1-5 alone; MOC1 epistatic over TE.
slr1 recessive mutant exhibited fewer tillers and taller stature.
SLR1-OE: tiller number was increased.
slr1 moc1 double: low tillers like moc1 alone (moc1 epistatic).
sd1 (GA20ox2 null) produces more tillers and shorter plants.
GA20ox1-OE line: fewer tillers and taller stature.
ipi1-1 CRISPR mutant: reduced IPA1 in shoot apex causing increased tillers.
Ub:IPI1-OE: increased IPA1 accumulation in shoot apex, reduced tillers.
shi1 mutant: dramatically reduced tiller number.
OsSHI1-OE: reduced plant height, decreased panicle, ectopic tillers at upper internodes but reduced normal tillers.
osmads57-1 activation-tagged: 28 tillers vs WT 10.
OsMADS57-antisense: reduced tillers.
OsMIR444a-OE lines: suppresses OsMADS57 expression, fewer tillers.
oscca1 mutant shows more tillers and delayed flowering.
OsCCA1-OE: fewer tillers than transgenic control.
OsPRR1-OE: reduced tiller numbers.
Knockdown of OsPIN1a decreases crown roots and increases tiller number.
OsPIN1b CRISPR mutant: increased tiller numbers, altered root gravitropism.
OsPIN2-OE leads to increased tiller numbers, tiller angle and shorter plant.
ospin9 knockout: decreased tillering.
OsPIN9-OE: increased tillering.
OsTIR1/OsAFB2 double mutants show severely compromised plant development including tillering defects.
japonica OsTB2-3bp+ allele: promotes axillary bud elongation, increased tillers.
reg1 allele of DST: reduced OsCKX2 expression, elevated CK, enhanced grain number and moderately increased tillers (Gn1a-independent +20% yield).
DST-OE: activates OsCKX2, reduced CK, reduced panicle branches and grain number; reduces tillers.
WT + 10uM GR24 completely inhibited the outgrowth of third leaf tiller buds.
d27 + 1.0 uM GR24 fully rescues tillering dwarf phenotype.
d10-2 + 1 uM GR24: sufficient to inhibit the outgrowth of first and second leaf buds; SL-biosynthesis-mutant responds.
d17 SL-biosynthesis mutant + GR24: phenotype rescued; SL-responsive.
d3-2 + 10uM GR24: tillers not suppressed; SL-insensitive.
d14 + GR24 treatment: axillary buds still grow (SL-insensitive).
d53 is resistant to exogenous application of GR24.
fc1 + 10uM GR24: third and fourth leaf tiller buds were not affected (SL-insensitive via FC1 requirement).
BAP (cytokinin) treatment suppresses FC1 expression and promotes tiller bud outgrowth.
Apical IAA application suppresses bud outgrowth via BRC1/FC1 induction.
NAA treatment rescued OsPIN1 RNAi phenotype and suppressed WT tillering.
NPA (auxin transport inhibitor, similar to TIBA) treatment largely rescued tiller number; blocks polar auxin transport.
GA (10^-5 M) 1 month treatment: decreased tiller number.
PBZ (paclobutrazol, GA inhibitor) significantly increased tiller number and decreased plant height.
High nitrogen fertilization causes downregulation of OsTB1 via NGR5-PRC2 H3K27me3 and promotes tiller formation.
Low nitrogen: reduced tillers, NGR5 not accumulated.
ngr5 + high N: no tiller response to nitrogen (N-insensitive) compared to ngr5 alone.
Low P conditions elevate SL biosynthesis genes D10/D17 and reduce tiller bud outgrowth.
ipa1-1D significantly suppressed the high tillering phenotype of d53.
ipa1-1D could repress the high-tillering phenotype of d27.
35S-FC1/d3-2 lines showed partial rescue of d3 defects (plant height, tiller number, stem thickness).
D53-RNAi in d3 background obviously reduced the tiller number.
D53-RNAi in d14 background: tiller number restored to nearly wild type levels.
d14 d53 double mutant showed dwarf tillering phenotype resembling d14 and d53 parental plants (no additive effect, epistatic).
d3 d53 double: dwarf tillering phenotype resembling d3; d3 phenotype epistatic over d53.
d27d10 double mutant showed similar tiller number and plant height to d10 (d10 epistatic).
lax1 moc1 double mutant shows enhanced suppression of axillary meristem formation.
Exogenous sucrose promotes D53 accumulation and tillering in rice.
Sucrose effect on tillering partially depends on D53; d53 does not further increase with sucrose.
Panicle removal (decapitation, DeP): increased sugar content, reduced OsCCA1 expression, threefold tiller increase.
Leaf removal (defoliation, DeF): decreased sugars, elevated OsCCA1, reduced tillers.
gt1 mutant: increased tiller bud outgrowth.
~110 kb deletion eliminating PROG1 cluster increased tiller number (domestication).
tig1 allele: pleiotropic effects including altered tiller number.
Knocking out OsAAP3 can promote growth of buds and effective tillers.
OsAAP5-OE: altered amino acid levels affect CK biosynthesis; reduced tillers.
gs1;2 mutant: reduced tiller bud growth due to reduced CK production.
osbzr1 loss: failure to recruit D53 to OsTB1 promoter; increased tiller outgrowth.
Exogenous BR stabilizes OsBZR1-D53 complex, representing OsTB1 and reducing tiller outgrowth.
Blocking of OsAAP7 promoted tillering and yield by determining basic and neutral amino acids accumulation.
osvil2 shows defective axillary bud outgrowth, reduced tillers.
Natural variation in Tn1a promoter increases rice tillering via TIF1 interaction.
dep1 mutant: reduced panicle length, reduced grain number, altered tiller number.
asn1 mutant reduces asparagine concentration and inhibits tiller outgrowth.
Overexpression of OsTB1 partially suppressed the tiller-overproducing phenotype of dwarf3 (d3).
d10 d3 double: high-tillering dwarf like each single mutant (parallel pathways converge).
d14 d53 double: resembling d14 and d53 parental plants.
Kinetin (cytokinin) treatment suppresses FC1 expression and promotes bud outgrowth.
BAP treatment clearly suppressed FC1 expression in a concentration-dependent manner and promoted bud outgrowth.
NPF7.7-OE increases effective tiller number by enhancing N uptake.
NPF7.1-OE promotes rice axillary buds outgrowth.
npf7.4 knockout promotes rice axillary buds outgrowth.
SL biosynthesis mutant combinations give similar high-tiller phenotype (d10 d14 etc).
fon1 mutant forms axillary buds normally but fails to elongate tiller buds.
osarf12 osarf17 osarf25 triple mutant: altered tiller angle and increased tillers.
OsIAA stabilized variants block auxin signaling, partially derepress bud outgrowth.
OsYUCCA mutants: reduced auxin synthesis affects crown roots and altered tiller dynamics.
OsTAR2 loss reduces IAA biosynthesis, affecting architecture and tiller number.
oslax1/2/3 triple mutant: strongly reduced axillary meristem formation.
d53 ipa1-1D double: IPA1 accumulation suppresses d53 high tillering.
Ub:IPI1 in ipa1-1D background: reverted thick culm and panicle size phenotypes of ipa1-1D (IPI1 dominant).
OsSHI1-OE/35S:IPA1-Flag double OE: recovered tiller development relative to 35S:IPA1 alone (OsSHI1 inhibits IPA1).
With or without BAP treatment, levels of FC1 mRNA in d3 and d10 were not significantly different; BAP acts independently of SL but d3 background already high-tillering.
T6P signaling (via TPS overexpression or sucrose) promotes bud outgrowth.
Exogenous orobanchol (natural rice SL) inhibits tiller outgrowth similar to GR24.
rac-GR24 synthetic SL: inhibits axillary bud outgrowth in WT.
Reducing D53 expression in d53 background drastically reduced the tiller number.
Wild-type Nipponbare produces characteristic 10-15 tillers at heading stage (baseline).
moc1 d10 double mutant: monoculm (moc1 epistatic over high-tillering d10).
osckx9 d27 double mutant: intermediate phenotype between singles.
Upon SL treatment, tiller number is unchanged in ipa1-10 (loss-of-function) and d14 ipa1-10; IPA1 required for SL-mediated tiller inhibition.
oscca1 d14 double: genetic data confirm D14 acts downstream; phenotype like d14.
oscca1 ostb1 double: phenotype resembles ostb1 single mutant (ostb1 epistatic).
shi1 ipa1 double: phenotype resembles ipa1 single (ipa1 downstream of shi1).
shi1 dep1 double: phenotype resembles dep1 single (dep1 downstream).
ohk4 ipa1 double: phenotype similar to ipa1; IPA1 is downstream of OsHK4 in panicle feedback.
OsRR21-OE in ohk4 background: partial rescue of panicle and tiller defects.
NGR5-OE + GA treatment: GA-induced NGR5 degradation partially counteracts OE effect.
NGR5-high-expression allele + high nitrogen: maximal tiller number and yield (synergy).
Under low N, d53 mutant still shows more tillers than WT; d53 partially insensitive to N-status.
Under N-deficient conditions, tiller number reduced in both WT and d3 and d10; N effect partially SL-independent.
ngr5 + GR24: SL signaling intact but N response absent; tillers reduced similar to GR24 alone (N-insensitive but SL-sensitive).
Shading reduces photosynthesis and sucrose, reducing tillers via increased OsCCA1.
Drought stress increases ABA and decreases tillers via NCED1 induction.
bzr1 d53 double: high-tillering like d53 (d53 epistatic for tiller count).
ngr5 + PBZ: GA blockade stabilizes SLR1 but no NGR5 to convert signal; moderate tiller increase via SLR1-MOC1 axis.
SLR1-OE resistant to GA degradation: retains high tiller phenotype with GA treatment.
Ammonium supply promotes tillers via OsPIN9 induction and CK production.
RCN127 F-box protein enhances rice tillering by mediating degradation of OsTB1 and OsTCP19.
osstp15 knockout: increased tiller number due to higher sugar content in shoot base.
miR396 OE targets OsGRF TFs affecting panicle and tillers indirectly.
Sucrose antagonizes GR24 inhibition of tillering by stabilizing D53.
Decapitation + GR24: GR24 partially blocks tiller outgrowth induced by decapitation (SL acts downstream of sugar signal).
d14 d53 + GR24: GR24 does not reduce tillers (SL signaling blocked at receptor + repressor levels).
SLR1-OE in tad1 background: tiller number additively elevated (both stabilize MOC1).
OsHK6 is cytokinin receptor; loss slightly reduces CK signaling and tillers.
OsRR type-A OE: negative feedback on CK signaling reduces tillers.
OsWOX4 plays role in maintaining axillary meristem after establishment; loss reduces tiller maintenance.
MP3 allele of OsTB1/FC1: moderately increased tiller/panicle number without compromising spikelet.
asn1 + high N: failed to increase tillers normally seen with high N (Asn essential for N signaling to buds).
osvil2 ostb1 double: complex phenotype, reduced overall tillers.
Cold stress: OsMADS57+OsTB1 switch to defense/cold mode, reducing D14 expression and tiller outgrowth.
ACC (ethylene precursor) treatment slightly reduces tiller outgrowth in rice.
OsIPT7-OE increases cytokinin levels and promotes tillering.
Takanari wild-type at 12th leaf: 10 tillers (4 primary, 6 secondary) baseline reference.
NIL-MP3 at 12th leaf: 13 tillers (5 primary, 8 secondary) +28% panicles vs Takanari.
NIL-fc1 at 12th leaf: 31 tillers (7 primary, 17 secondary, 7 tertiary) +143% panicles vs Takanari; thinner lighter tillers cause lodging.
NIL-MP3 in P-deficient soil still tended to produce more tillers than Takanari.
MP3 further enhances above-ground biomass by 3.5% at elevated CO2 (579 umol/mol).
OsYUCCA1-OE elevates auxin, reduces tillers via enhanced apical dominance.
Tiller number of double mutant osvil2 ostb1 was similar to that of ostb1, suggesting that osvil2 is epistatic to ostb1.
OsGATA8-L haplotype shows reduced productive tiller proportion via failure to repress OsTCP19.
The OsGATA8-H haplotype is identified as a high-NUE haplotype with enhanced nitrogen uptake and a higher proportion of productive tillers.
CRISPR/Cas9 mutants of Os1900 (and Os5100) show altered SL biosynthesis and tillering responses.
Os1900 promoter mutations alone can increase tiller numbers and grain yields even under minor-fertilizer conditions.
Deficiency of DTN1 results in reduced photosynthetic rate and decreased contents of sucrose; reduced tiller number in dtn1 mutant.
Reduced tiller number in dtn1 mutant could be partially rescued by exogenous sucrose treatment.
Overexpression of NGR5 in the dtn1-2 could partially rescue the reduced tiller number.
Researchers identified a high-tillering mutant tillering20 (t20), which could be restored to the wild type by treatment with the strigolactone analog rac-GR24.
t20 high tillering could be restored to the wild type by treatment with the strigolactone analog rac-GR24.
ABA treatment significantly reduced the number of basal tillers in both t20 and wild-type plants.
ABA treatment significantly reduced the number of basal tillers in both t20 and wild-type plants.
Overexpression of OsDRM1 in transgenic plants delayed axillary bud outgrowth by suppressing cell division within the buds.
The plant heights of ohk3 ohk4 and ohk4 ohk5 double mutants were slightly but significantly reduced, while the tiller numbers varied.
Excessive nitrogen promotes the formation of nonproductive tillers; OsGATA8-L (loss) accessions show reduced productive tillering even under high N.
Os1900 mutants escape N-induced SL biosynthesis and remain high-tillering even under high fertilizer.
